statement.markdown: Given an integer `n`, return `true` if it is a perfect square, otherwise return `false`. An integer is a perfect square if it can be expressed as the square of some integer, i.e., `n == x * x` for some integer `x`. You can use the `sqrt` function from the math library to get the square root of a number.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the input number is a perfect square
statement.notes: You can use the `sqrt` function to get the square root of a number.

examples.explanation: There is no integer square root for 20

hints.text: Rethink the condition for a perfect square

editorial.title: Editorial: Perfect Square Validation
editorial.intuition: Check if the square of an integer is equal to the input number
editorial.bruteForce: Check all numbers up to the square root of the input number
editorial.optimalSolution: Check if the square root of the input number is an integer
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The square root of a perfect square is an integer
editorial.dryRun: Test the solution with some examples
